questions.text: Friction always acts in the direction:
questions.explanation: Friction always opposes motion. It acts in the direction opposite to the direction of motion.

questions.text: Rolling friction is less than sliding friction.
questions.explanation: Rolling friction is less than sliding friction. That's why wheels are used for easy movement.

questions.text: Why do we sprinkle powder on carrom board?
questions.correctAnswer: To reduce friction between striker/board and make movement smoother.
questions.explanation: Powder fills irregularities and reduces friction, allowing smooth movement of striker and coins.

questions.text: Friction depends on the ________ of surfaces in contact.
questions.explanation: Friction depends on the nature/roughness of surfaces. Rougher surfaces have more friction.
